user_query:
I’m a criminal justice professor trying to keep tabs on official evidence around the government’s “payment-by-results” approach. Please build a small, event-triggered tool that monitors the workspace directory watch/ for new .txt files and, whenever a new one appears, runs a focused web collection job as follows.

Inputs and trigger:
- Watch the local folder watch/ for new files with extension .txt.
- Each non-empty line in a new .txt file is a search theme (e.g., “probation evaluation”).
- For this run, process the provided file watch/seed_queries.txt as an initial event (treat it as if it just appeared on startup). The tool must not reprocess the same input file twice across runs; persist processed state locally.

Search and download rules (Internet required):
- For each theme, build a search query that appends the phrase "payment by results" to the theme and use a general web search engine (no paid APIs) to get results.
- Only follow result links from official UK sources whose domains end with one of: gov.uk, justice.gov.uk, parliament.uk, justiceinspectorates.gov.uk, police.uk, nhs.uk.
- For each theme, download up to the first 3 eligible result pages. Save the raw search results page HTML as evidence for that theme.

Extraction and outputs (under outputs/{batch_id}/):
- Batch ID: use the input filename stem (e.g., seed_queries for watch/seed_queries.txt).
- Save the raw search results page HTML to outputs/{batch_id}/search/{theme_slug}.html (one per theme).
- Save each downloaded page’s raw HTML to outputs/{batch_id}/pages/{theme_slug}_{index}.html and extract readable text to outputs/{batch_id}/text/{theme_slug}_{index}.txt.
- Create outputs/{batch_id}/summary.csv with columns exactly: batch_id, input_file, theme, page_index, source_url, source_domain, local_html_path, local_text_path, html_sha256, text_word_count, retrieved_at_iso, search_results_html_path. One row per downloaded page.
- Persist a state file at outputs/state/processed_batches.json that records batch_id, input_file path, processed_at ISO timestamp, and the count of themes processed. The tool must use this state to avoid reprocessing an input file on subsequent runs.
- If a theme yields no eligible links, still save its search results HTML and write a note to outputs/{batch_id}/log.txt; summary.csv simply has no row for that theme.

Verification constraints:
- The number of rows in outputs/{batch_id}/summary.csv must equal the number of files under outputs/{batch_id}/text/.
- For every row, text_word_count must equal the word count of the corresponding extracted text file, and html_sha256 must match the SHA-256 of the corresponding saved raw HTML file.
- All output paths referenced in summary.csv must exist on disk.

Please provide whatever script(s) are needed to run the watcher locally, and ensure it processes watch/seed_queries.txt on first run, then remains able to process any future .txt dropped into watch/ without reprocessing already-completed batches. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
